scaffold_text:
Before I draft the provisional 5‑day plan, a few quick clarifying questions:

1. Do you mean 5 full days in Tokyo (five full days on the ground), or 5 calendar days including arrival/departure travel time?  
2. Are your travel dates flexible next month (July 2026), or do you already have specific departure/return dates?  
3. What style of accommodation do you prefer: budget (hostel/capsule), mid-range (3-star hotel / private Airbnb), or comfortable/upscale?  
4. Any must-see items or activities (e.g., museums, teamLab, sumo, sushi breakfast, day trip to Hakone/Kamakura)? Any mobility or dietary restrictions?  
5. Would you like help booking flights/hotels or just the itinerary and cost estimates?

Assumptions (I’ll use these in the provisional plan; say if you want changes)
- “5‑day” means 5 full days in Tokyo (arrive night before or early morning Day 1, depart after Day 5). Tell me if you want arrival/departure to eat into those days.  
- Departing San Francisco (SFO) and returning there. Economy roundtrip flight. Travel in July 2026 (peak summer).  
- Traveler is single, healthy adult, comfortable with mid-range accommodations (3-star / business hotel) in central Tokyo (Shinjuku, Shibuya, Ueno, or Asakusa area).  
- No JR Rail Pass (not cost-effective for Tokyo-only), local transit with Suica/Pasmo card.  
- Moderate pace: mix of classic sights, neighborhoods, one cultural activity, one skyline/view, and one lighter day trip option.  
- No major events (concerts, festivals requiring special tickets) that would dramatically change availability or prices.  
- All prices below are estimates, shown as “Estimate.”

Clarify any of the above before I finalize; if you’re ok, I’ll proceed with a provisional budget‑aware 5‑day plan. Below is a provisional plan based on the assumptions.

Provisional 5‑Day Tokyo Itinerary (one traveler) — Budget-aware
Summary budget estimate (rounded):
- Flights (roundtrip SFO ↔ Tokyo, economy): Estimate $900  
- Accommodation (5 nights, mid-range business hotel): Estimate $700 ($140/night)  
- Airport transfers (roundtrip): Estimate $40  
- Local transport (subway/short trains / Suica top-ups): Estimate $50  
- Food (mix of convenience store, casual restaurants, mid-range dinners): Estimate $250 ($50/day)  
- Attractions & activities (museum entry, views, teamLab or similar, cultural experience): Estimate $150  
- eSIM/local data or pocket Wi‑Fi: Estimate $20  
- Travel insurance (short trip): Estimate $70  
- Misc / souvenirs / contingency: Estimate $120  
Total estimated budget: $2,300 (Estimate)
Buffer to $2,500: $200 retained as extra cushion for higher flight/hotel rates, taxis, or optional splurges.

Day-by-day plan (times are suggestions; use Suica/Pasmo for transit)
Day 0 — Travel / Arrival (if arriving morning of Day 1, shift accordingly)
- Fly SFO → Tokyo (overnight flight typical). (Flight Estimate: $900 roundtrip)  
- Arrival at Haneda or Narita. Transfer to hotel (Haneda is quicker and cheaper). (Airport transfer Estimate: $20 roundtrip if Haneda; up to $40 if Narita)  
- Check in, short rest, walk nearby neighborhood to get oriented. Grab dinner at an izakaya or convenience store bite. (Dinner Estimate: $10–25)

Day 1 — Classic Tokyo: Asakusa, Ueno, Akihabara
- Morning: Senso-ji temple in Asakusa. Walk Nakamise shopping street — cheap souvenirs, snacks. (Free to visit; small purchases Estimate: $10)  
- Lunch: Try tempura or tendon at a local shop (Estimate: $10–15).  
- Afternoon: Walk or take short subway to Ueno Park (museums, park, Shinobazu Pond). Option: Tokyo National Museum or National Museum of Nature and Science (entry Estimate: $6–10).  
- Late afternoon: Akihabara — electronics, anime shops, maid café (optional special experience extra cost).  
- Evening: Dinner in Akihabara/Shinjuku; explore Shinjuku Golden Gai or Omoide Yokocho for inexpensive drinks and atmosphere. (Dinner + drink Estimate: $20–35)  
- Daily transport & small entries Estimate: $15

Day 2 — Shibuya, Harajuku, Meiji Shrine, Omotesando
- Morning: Meiji Shrine (free). Walk through Yoyogi Park.  
- Late morning: Takeshita Street (Harajuku) for youth fashion and crepes (snacks).  
- Lunch: Casual ramen or curry in Harajuku area (Estimate: $10–15).  
- Afternoon: Omotesando for architecture and boutique window-shopping; visit teamLab Planets or teamLab Borderless alternative if you want digital art (teamLab entry Estimate: $30–35). If you skip, visit Nezu Museum or Mori Art Museum (Roppongi).  
- Evening: Shibuya crossing, Hachiko statue, rooftop or Sky View bar for skyline (if paid entrance like Shibuya Sky, Estimate: $20–30). Dinner in Shibuya (Estimate: $20–35).  
- Day estimate (transport + attraction + meals): $60

Day 3 — Tsukiji Outer Market, Ginza, Tokyo Tower / Roppongi
- Early morning: Tsukiji Outer Market for sushi breakfast or seafood bowl (kaisendon) — excellent value if you like sushi (Estimate: $20–35).  
- Morning/Noon: Walk or subway to Ginza for department stores, affordable lunch sets (teishoku) and window-shopping.  
- Afternoon: Roppongi Hills Mori Tower observation or Tokyo Tower/Tokyo Midtown for views (Observation Estimate: $15–25). Option: Mori Art Museum (if not earlier).  
- Evening: Optional sumo stable morning visit is usually very early and requires booking — if interested, I can check availability. Otherwise enjoy an izakaya dinner in Roppongi (Estimate: $25–40).  
- Day estimate: $70

Day 4 — Optional day trip (budget-aware choices)
Option A: Kamakura (temples, giant Buddha) — easy day trip by train (~1 hour).  
- Transport to Kamakura: JR or private train (no JR Pass; use local tickets; Estimate roundtrip $15–20). Entry donations/temple fees minimal. Lunch + café (Estimate: $20).  
Option B: Hakone (views of Mt. Fuji, onsen) — more expensive and time-consuming; expect $60–100 for transport and attractions.  
Option C: Stay in Tokyo and explore Odaiba (teamLab Planets or waterfront), Tokyo Skytree (Observation Estimate $20–30) and evening Odaiba sunset.  
- I’ll assume Kamakura for cost-effectiveness. Day estimate: $60

Day 5 — Shopping, neighborhoods, last-minute sights
- Morning: Visit Yanaka Ginza for old-town Tokyo feel and final souvenirs (cheap snacks, small shops).  
- Lunch: Conveyor belt sushi or set meal (Estimate: $15–25).  
- Afternoon: Free time to revisit favorite spot, last-minute shopping in Shinjuku or Akihabara. Consider a short cruise on Sumida River if you want a calm view (optional small fee).  
- Evening: Final dinner — consider an upgraded sushi dinner or a memorable izakaya (Estimate: $40–80 if splurging; budget $25 if keeping economical).  
- Transfer to airport for late-night flight or stay one more night depending on flight time. (If same-day return, allow 3+ hours before flight at the airport.)

Practical, budget-aware tips and extras
- Suica/Pasmo smartcard: top up $30–50 to cover subway and small purchases (Estimate included in local transport).  
- eSIM or pocket Wi‑Fi: buy eSIM online before departure (~$10–25) or rent pocket Wi‑Fi (more expensive). (Estimate: $20)  
- Choose business hotels or chain hotels (Toyoko Inn, APA, Richmond) for comfortable private rooms around $100–160/night. Capsule hotels and hostels can reduce cost to $40–80/night if you want to save.  
- Eat like locals to save: convenience store meals, standing sushi/bento lunch sets, and lunchtime set menus (teishoku) are great values.  
- Buy attraction tickets online in advance for discounted/guaranteed entry (teamLab and some views sell out).  
- Carry a bit of cash — many small shops and older temples accept cash only. ATMs at 7‑Eleven accept foreign cards.

Detailed estimated cost breakdown (rounded, labelled as Estimate)
- Flights (SFO ↔ Tokyo, economy): Estimate $900  
- Accommodation (5 nights mid-range): Estimate $700 ($140/night)  
- Airport transfers: Estimate $40  
- Local transport (subway / trains / Suica top-ups): Estimate $50  
- Food (5 days): Estimate $250  
- Attractions & activities (entries, 1 teamLab or Sky, museum fees): Estimate $150  
- eSIM / connectivity: Estimate $20  
- Travel insurance: Estimate $70  
- Misc / souvenirs / contingency: Estimate $120  
Grand total estimate: $2,300 (Estimate). Comfortable cushion up to your $2,500 budget.

If you want a lower-cost option
- Swap mid-range hotel for capsule or hostel: saves ~$400 ($300–400 total for 5 nights). New estimated total ≈ $1,900–2,000.  
- Skip paid attractions (teamLab / paid observation decks) and dine cheaper: save $50–100.

Next steps / what I need from you to finalize
- Confirm whether you want 5 full days in Tokyo (yes/no) and your flexibility on travel dates in July.  
- Tell me accommodation preference (budget / mid-range / comfy).  
- Indicate any must-see(s) or if you want a day trip to Hakone vs Kamakura vs stay-in-city.  
Once you confirm those, I’ll provide finalized daily schedule with tightened time slots, exact public transit routes, and a refined budget with booking tips and links.

**Day 1: Arrival in Tokyo & Shinjuku Exploration**
*   **Morning/Afternoon:** Arrive at Narita (NRT) or Haneda (HND) Airport. Clear immigration, pick up a Pocket WiFi or SIM card at the airport, and purchase a **Suica or Pasmo card**. Take the **Narita Express (N'EX)** or Keisei Skyliner (to Ueno) or the Limousine Bus/Monorail from Haneda to central Tokyo.
*   **Evening:** Check into your accommodation. Head to **Shinjuku**.
    *   Experience the iconic, bustling **Shibuya Crossing** (one stop away on the JR Yamanote Line).
    *   Return to Shinjuku for dinner in the **Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane)** or a more modern izakaya under the train tracks in **Piss Alley (Shinjuku Golden Gai)** for yakitori and a drink.
    *   **Budget Tip:** Your first meal is a great chance for affordable, delicious yakitori.

**Day 2: Historic Tokyo & Pop Culture**
*   **Morning:** Travel to **Asakusa**. Visit the famous **Senso-ji Temple**, Tokyo's oldest temple. Explore the Nakamise-dori street leading to the temple for traditional snacks and souvenirs.
*   **Afternoon:** Walk across the Sumida River to the **Tokyo Skytree**. You don't need to go up (expensive); instead, visit the free observatory in the **Asahi Beer Hall** (the "Golden Flame" building) for a great view *of* the Skytree.
*   **Evening:** Head to **Akihabara** (Electric Town). Immerse yourself in anime, manga, and gaming culture. Explore multi-story arcades, electronic shops, and niche hobby stores. Have a casual dinner in the area (e.g., a themed cafe or a simple tonkatsu place).

**Day 3: Imperial Majesty & Modern Art**
*   **Morning:** Visit the **East Gardens of the Imperial Palace**. (Check opening days; closed Mon/Fri). The gardens are vast, beautiful, and free to enter.
*   **Afternoon:** Walk or take a short train to the **Ginza** district. Window-shop at luxury boutiques and flagship stores. For lunch, head to the basement food halls (depachika) of department stores like Mitsukoshi for an incredible array of ready-to-eat gourmet foods—a perfect solo traveler experience.

scaffold_text:
# Tokyo 5-Day Itinerary — Solo Traveler from San Francisco

---

## Before the Plan: Clarifying Questions

I have several questions that would meaningfully change the recommendations. I'll note how each affects the plan.

1. **Travel dates?** "Next month" affects flight prices significantly. A mid-week departure (Tue/Wed) is typically cheaper than weekend. Are there specific dates?
2. **Accommodation style?** Capsule hotel (~$30–50/night), budget business hotel (~$80–120/night), or mid-range (~$150–200/night)? This is the biggest budget lever after flights.
3. **Food priorities?** Convenience store meals and ramen vs. sit-down restaurants and izakayas vs. occasional splurge dining?
4. **Activity interests?** Temples/history, anime/pop culture, nightlife, day trips, shopping, food tours, museums?
5. **Physical mobility/comfort?** Tokyo involves a *lot* of walking (10–15 miles/day is common). Any limitations?
6. **Visa status?** US passport holders get 90-day visa-free entry to Japan — assuming this applies.
7. **Travel insurance?** Strongly recommended for Japan. Do you have coverage?
8. **Credit cards?** Japan is still heavily cash-based. Do you have a no-foreign-transaction-fee card or plan to use ATMs?
9. **Any dietary restrictions?** Affects restaurant planning meaningfully.
10. **Is the $2,500 a hard cap or a target?** Knowing the flexibility matters for recommendations.

---

## Stated Assumptions

Since I don't have your answers, I'm building the plan on the following assumptions. **If any are wrong, the budget will shift.**

| Assumption | Detail |
|---|---|
| Travel dates | Mid-week departure, mid-June (not a Japanese holiday) |
| Traveler | Solo adult, US passport holder, no mobility restrictions |
| Accommodation | Budget business hotel (think Toyoko Inn or similar) |
| Food style | Mix: convenience store breakfasts, casual lunches, sit-down dinners |
| Interests | Balanced: culture, food, neighborhoods, some pop culture |
| Flight booking | Booked ~3–4 weeks out (not ideal; earlier is better) |
| Currency | Assumes ~150 JPY per $1 USD (verify current rate) |
| Visa | Visa-free entry, 90 days |
| Travel insurance | **Not included** in budget — strongly recommend adding ~$50–80 |
| Local transport | IC card (Suica/Pasmo) for trains and buses |
| No day trips | Staying within Greater Tokyo to control costs |

---

## Budget Overview (Estimates)

> ⚠️ All figures are **estimates** based on publicly available pricing as of early 2025. Actual costs will vary.

| Category | Estimated Cost (USD) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| ✈️ Round-trip flight (SFO–NRT) | $700–$950 | Economy, mid-week, ~3–4 weeks out |
| 🏨 Hotel (5 nights) | $400–$550 | ~$80–110/night, budget business hotel |
| 🍜 Food & drink (5 days) | $200–$300 | ~$40–60/day mixed strategy |
| 🚆 Local transport (IC card) | $50–$70 | Trains, buses; no bullet train |
| 🎟️ Activities & entrance fees | $100–$150 | Mix of free and paid |
| 🛍️ Shopping & souvenirs | $150–$200 | Flexible; easiest to cut |
| 📶 Pocket WiFi or SIM card | $30–$50 | 5-day rental or eSIM |
| 🧾 Miscellaneous (tips, coin lockers, etc.) | $50–$75 | Japan has no tipping culture |
| **Total Estimated Range** | **$1,680–$2,345** | **Leaves $155–$820 buffer** |

> 💡 **The flight is the biggest variable.** Prices from SFO to Tokyo (NRT or HND) fluctuate widely. Check Google Flights, Kayak, and directly with ANA/JAL. If the flight runs $950+, compress the shopping budget first.

---

## Pre-Trip Checklist

- [ ] Book flight ASAP — prices rise closer to departure
